dict.string: Used to open Terminal so you can run `claude /login` when adding an account.
Copyright © 2026 Thanh Ngô. Licensed under MIT.
Claude Bar 11.5 — menu-bar manager for multiple Claude Code accounts

Rename profile now works on the first Save. Clicking the three-dot menu next to an account → Rename… used to race the menu-bar popover dismissal: the first Save click would close both popovers and skip the rename, only the retry stuck. The rename form now lives in a dedicated floating window (same pattern as the Add-account wizard) so its state is fully decoupled from the popover lifecycle. Web usage scrape now reports each window correctly. Anthropic added per-model weekly bars (Weekly · Opus / Sonnet / Haiku) alongside the all-models bar, and the previous DOM walk was matching the wrong wrapper — accounts with Opus pinned at 100 % rendered 7d as 100 % even with headroom, and certain compact layouts collapsed 5h and 7d to identical numbers. Scraping now anchors on each progressbar, classifies its smallest labelling ancestor, and drops per-model weekly blocks so the headline limit is what you see.

Fixed Rename profile silently failing on the first attempt (issue #7). Fixed 5h and 7d usage windows showing the wrong percentages — per-model weekly bars hijacking the 7d reading, and compact layouts collapsing both windows to the same value (issue #6).

The menu-bar popover still dismisses when the Rename window opens (macOS menu-bar app behaviour, not preventable without giving up the dropdown style). The rename itself goes through on Save; reopen the menu to see the new name. Existing v11.x Google tokens are still missing the `spreadsheets` scope shipped in 11.3. The cb_gsheets_* tools return Google "insufficient permission" until you Disconnect + Reconnect the Google connector — that mints a fresh token with the new scope set. Drive / Calendar / Gmail tools keep working on the old scope until you do.
